records.ety: [L. dehortari; de- + hortari to urge, exhort.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Dehorted; p. pr. & vb. n. Dehorting.]

defs: To urge to abstain or refrain; to dissuade.
quotes: The apostles vehemently dehort us from unbelief.
“Exhort” remains, but dehort, a word whose place neither “dissuade” nor any other exactly supplies, has escaped us.
